Kakadu (Template).name: Kakadu National Park — Simulated Survey Data

shocks.label: 2021-Dry
shocks.event: Extended drought — dry season 3 weeks longer than historical average

shocks.label: 2023-Dry
shocks.event: Record low water table — emergent marsh dominance exceeds 35%

Kakadu (Template).narrative: Kakadu shows a classic deceptive drift pattern: complexity declining steadily from K_eff=5.8 to 4.5 over six years while seasonal velocity stays moderate. Emergent marsh is quietly absorbing hectares from open water and floating vegetation — the site is homogenizing toward a single dominant habitat type. The seasonal wet/dry oscillation masks the trend on any single survey, but the multi-year complexity trace reveals progressive structural concentration. By 2023, the dry-season carrier is dominated by emergent marsh (38%) with open water collapsed to 8%. This is the forward signal: the site is losing habitat diversity behind apparent seasonal stability.

Doñana (Template).name: Doñana National Park — Simulated Survey Data
Doñana (Template).narrative: Doñana presents textbook deceptive drift. Complexity declines so gradually (6.2 → 5.7 over six years) that no single survey triggers alarm. Velocity never spikes — the surface looks perfectly calm. But the steady downward slope on the complexity trace tells the story: this site is quietly losing structural diversity. The velocity meter reads calm. The complexity trace says otherwise. This is exactly the pattern HUF-GOV was built to detect.
